_spec.purpose: Automation-change trail. Phase 12 (AUTOMATION RETRO + UPGRADE) appends one entry per machine upgrade made after a run, both reactive fixes and frontier improvements: engine scripts, helpers, prompts, agent definitions. gmail_draft.py renders this run's entries into every dated Gmail draft so the maintainer can monitor the machine's evolution daily and pinpoint which week introduced a regression. Upgrades are committed separately (message prefix 'upgrade(<date>):') so a revert of that one commit rolls the change back cleanly.
rules: Never weaken a gate, threshold, or hard-fail rule; upgrades tighten, repair, or automate.
Every engine/script change is verified before commit (this run's slides + examples/demo-deck through render+qa, plus a defect-reconstruction test for new gates).
0-3 upgrades per run; zero is acceptable and is recorded as nothing (the email section then reads 'no changes').
Frontier improvements never displace a needed fix: the 0-3 budget fills reactive-first.

entry_schema.kind: fix (from this run's deviations) | improvement (from the frontier scan)
entry_schema.area: engine | scripts | assets | prompts | agents | knowledge
entry_schema.change: one sentence, what the machine now does differently
entry_schema.trigger: the run deviation that motivated it, or the source URL for a frontier improvement
entry_schema.verification: what was tested and the result
entry_schema.rollback: how to undo (usually: revert the named commit)

scan_log_schema.focus: which rotation area was scanned (must differ from the last 3 runs)
scan_log_schema.found: one-line summary of the most relevant findings

entries.change: qa.py now detects text-on-text overprint: per-line box intersection across distinct elements, FAIL for primary text pairs, WARN when decorative or data-overlap-ok is involved; render.py extracts per-line rects, DOM ancestry, and the new data-overlap-ok intent attribute, and data-decorative/data-overlap-ok now inherit from ancestor containers.
entries.trigger: Run 1 shipped-then-caught hard fail: slide-3 body copy rendered over the 'URBAN ALASKA GRID PEAK' bar label; machine QA passed it because each element sat in its own safe zone, and only the scorer's transcription caught it (the pixel-critic subagents were session-limit-blocked).
entries.verification: Reconstructed pre-fix slide-3 FAILS with 'text collision (100% overprint)'; the 9 shipped slides pass with 0 fails (cover chip demoted to WARN via data-overlap-ok); examples/demo-deck passes clean.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-08) commit on PR #3 (tsturg/modest-mccarthy-g7otcl)

entries.change: bootstrap.sh probes that pypdf actually IMPORTS (in a subprocess) rather than merely exists, and repairs the Debian cryptography-41 pyo3 panic by upgrading cryptography; dependency probe also catches non-ImportError import crashes.
entries.trigger: Run 1: assemble.py's vector-PDF path crashed mid-run on 'pyo3_runtime.PanicException' from the system cryptography package; recovered only by a manual pip upgrade.
entries.verification: bootstrap.sh runs clean end-to-end on this container and reports 'pypdf import: ok'; assemble.py produced the run's vector PDF after the same fix applied manually.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-08) commit on PR #3 (tsturg/modest-mccarthy-g7otcl)

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/akgeo.js: AKGeo.alaskaProjection (canonical fit) and AKGeo.zoomTo (baseline full-state fit, then scale-and-retranslate regional zoom) so zoomed maps stop re-discovering the fitExtent-to-small-bbox trap; documented in SKILL.md with the stroke-only-at-zoom rule.
entries.trigger: Run 1: slides 2 and 7 both rendered a giant fill disc + mis-scaled map from fitExtent on a small lon/lat bbox and had to be rebuilt mid-run with the manual recipe.
entries.verification: Recipe is the exact code that fixed slides 2 and 7 in the shipped deck (both pass QA and were visually verified at full size).
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-08) commit on PR #3 (tsturg/modest-mccarthy-g7otcl)

entries.change: New Phase 12 AUTOMATION RETRO + UPGRADE in the master routine (analyze run vs spec, implement 0-3 verified bounded upgrades, log here, commit separately as 'upgrade(<date>):'), and gmail_draft.py now renders an 'Automation changes this run' section from this ledger into every dated draft.
entries.trigger: Maintainer request after run 1: make the automation self-improving with a daily-monitorable, dated, rollback-able trail of what changed.
entries.verification: gmail_draft.py re-run against the shipped run 1 artifacts renders the new section with these entries; payload builds clean.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-08) commit on PR #3 (tsturg/modest-mccarthy-g7otcl)

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/aktype.js: AK.fitText(el, {min, max, maxLines}) binary-searches a display headline's font-size until it renders in maxLines line boxes with no overflow (clamps to min + sets data-fit-overflow=1 if the box is genuinely too small), so a large headline can never silently soft-wrap an extra line into the block below it; wired into DESIGN_DOCTRINE section 2 and SKILL.md as the one fit-to-box implementation for future decks.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-09 recurring #1 defect: slides 1, 4, 7, 8 each set a large Archivo headline in a container narrower than its longest line; each soft-wrapped an extra line and machine_qa FAILED it as a text-overprint, all four repaired by hand. The doctrine prescribed a binary-search fit-to-box recipe but there was no shared helper, so every deck re-implemented or forgot it.
entries.verification: Defect reconstruction (80px headline in a 560px box, no helper) FAILS qa.py with 'text collision (53% overprint)' at 5 line boxes; identical markup WITH AK.fitText({min:40,max:80,maxLines:3}) converges to 58.5px / 3 line boxes and PASSES qa.py. This run's slides (out/2026-07-09/slides) render OK, qa WARN, 0 fails (unchanged from baseline); examples/demo-deck renders OK, qa PASS (unchanged). No gate weakened; no new runtime dependency (vanilla offline JS); shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-09) commit on branch tsturg/festive-darwin-c9jczw (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.change: qa.py now emits a WARN-level 'busy art under text' tripwire: for each PRIMARY text line box (font_px>=30) it samples the rendered PNG, masks the glyph ink (plus a 2px dilation), and warns when the remaining background carries high-contrast structured edges (bg edge density >= 0.03) -- i.e. a canvas/bitmap arc or texture crossing text that the DOM-only collision gate cannot see. WARN only; it points the pixel critics at a box and never FAILs.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-10 clearest gate blind spot: machine_qa PASSED all 9 slides (0/0) yet a canvas flightpath arc crossed two S3 body lines and a canvas orbit arc crossed the S4 headline; only the pixel critics caught them, because text_collisions() compares DOM/SVG text against DOM/SVG text and canvas ink is a bitmap invisible to render.py's DOM walk.
entries.verification: Reconstruction (body copy over a bright canvas arc + dashed grid) WARNs at bg edge density 0.04 while its clean control (same arc routed above the text) stays silent -> the tripwire discriminates the defect. This run's 9 shipped slides stay PASS 0 fails 0 warns (no false positives on the real, well-composed deck). examples/demo-deck still exits 0 (PASS -> WARN via one sensible warn on slide-03's display line over the generative structure field; no new FAILs). Clean-slide noise floor measured at <=0.010 vs threshold 0.03. No gate weakened (WARN only), no new runtime dependency (numpy/PIL already required by qa.py).
entries.commit: 64d950baf6e8c0eeff1701fd61ede96f941b95ca

entries.change: New scripts/style_lint.py: a pre-flight CLI that applies site_build.py's exact prose-colon rule (byte-for-byte replica: strip script/style, tags->newlines, drop URLs and clock times, then any remaining ':' on a line is a violation) to arbitrary text via stdin, --file, or --file X.json --json-field FIELD (dot-path; '.' lints every string). Exits 1 with the offending line. Phase 3.5 and Phase 6 of the routine now lint the docket note and copy.json emitted fields with it BEFORE ship.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-10 repeat friction: site_build's prose-colon ship gate fired TWICE in Phase 11 (a docket history note, then copy.json first_comment 'Sources for today's deck:'), forcing manual rephrase at ship; run 2026-07-09 hit the same class twice. FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-09 parked a pre-flight colon lint. The ship gate is correct and stays unchanged; this only moves the catch earlier.
entries.verification: style_lint FAILs (exit 1) on both of this run's original ship-time failing strings (the first_comment colon line and a '- Update:' docket-note line); PASSes the fixed copy.json first_comment field; exempts clock times (4:30) and URLs exactly as site_build does; a direct cross-check confirms colon_violations() matches site_build.prose_colon_gate on clock/URL/ratio cases. No gate touched or weakened; no new dependency.
entries.commit: 64d950baf6e8c0eeff1701fd61ede96f941b95ca

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/aklabel.js: AK.canvasLabel(cx,x,y,text,{color,align}) draws any canvas-rendered label with an opaque knockout plate under the glyphs (auto-picking a near-black/near-white plate from the text luminance), so an in-scene label's contrast depends on (text,plate) only and never on the art beneath; AK.rectsOverlap keeps stacked labels from merging. Documented in SKILL.md. Opt-in; DOM/SVG text still preferred where layout allows.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-11 dominant defect class: machine_qa PASSED 0/0 yet the pixel critics caught ~10 canvas/decorative labels that were illegible -- S4 mono readouts ~1.5:1 on the ochre strata band, S6 SUSPENDED tag, S7 two flag labels overprinting into 'NSTARCORP', S8 context line on ochre. Canvas-drawn text is a bitmap with no DOM node, so text_collisions(), contrast_estimate(), and the 2026-07-10 busy-art tripwire (all of which walk render.py's DOM text_nodes) are structurally blind to it; the deck was repaired by hand with inline knockout chips. This commits that hand-fix as reusable machinery. (A canvas-text-aware QA gate was assessed and PARKED: not safely boundable without OCR/false-positives on dense sectional artwork -- FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-11.)
entries.verification: Reconstruction over an fbm-mottled ochre band (#B0803A) with the exact failing text/colours: RAW cx.fillText label reads ~1.9:1 (reproduces the shipped-then-caught defect) and RAW ENSTAR/HILCORP merge into 'ENSTAHILCORP' at ~2.8:1; the SAME labels via AK.canvasLabel read ~8.5:1 (bg = plate rgb(26,25,23), independent of the ochre) and ~12.9:1, and AK.rectsOverlap keeps them apart -- confirmed visually and by a pixel sampler using qa.py's contrast method. Regression: out/2026-07-11/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 and examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (the single known 2026-07-10 slide-03 busy-art warn), both unchanged (new asset is unreferenced). qa.py on the verification slide: PASS 0/0 (no false positives). No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency (vanilla offline JS); shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-11) commit 814b8c4 on PR #22 (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.trigger: Maintainer directive (2026-07-11 dev session): take the studio from 2D to 3D and make the illustration world class. Empirical probe overturned the 'WebGL experimental' assumption: SwiftShader/ANGLE renders a full PBR frame at 2160x2700 in ~70ms in this container.
entries.change: GPU PBR bench: committed three.module.min.js (r170, MIT) + new assets/js/akthree.js (deterministic scene kit: ACES + PCFSoft shadow rig, procedural IBL softbox room via PMREMGenerator, brand material presets gold/steel/clay/plastic/ice/emissive, arcticNight/goldenHour/galleryWhite lighting rigs, lathe/tube/extrude geometry helpers, webglOK probe + black-frame snapshot sentinel). Includes the pixelRatio-before-setSize fix that a hand-rolled integration would silently miss (renders at 1x).
entries.verification: examples/proof-3d slide-01/03 render PASS with 0 errors (3.4-8.7s incl full pipeline); sentinel verified reading 24 px variance; regression: examples/demo-deck (baseline WARN unchanged) and runs/2026-07-11 slides (PASS) re-rendered clean with the new assets present.

entries.trigger: Maintainer directive (2026-07-11 dev session): take the studio from 2D to 3D and make the illustration world class. Empirical probe overturned the 'WebGL experimental' assumption: SwiftShader/ANGLE renders a full PBR frame at 2160x2700 in ~70ms in this container.
entries.change: CPU SDF raymarcher: new assets/js/aksdf.js (sphere tracing, quadratic smin, twist/repeat/fbm displacement, tetrahedral normals, single-ray soft shadows, 5-tap AO applied to indirect terms only per the Quilez rule, fresnel rim, two-tone ramp, ACES out; deadlineMs graceful degradation) for organic sculpted heroes meshes cannot do.
entries.verification: examples/proof-3d slide-02 renders a blended fbm-displaced monument with shadows+AO in 7.9s at 540x675 internal (inside the 30s cap with full quality flags true); qa PASS.

entries.trigger: Maintainer directive (2026-07-11 dev session): take the studio from 2D to 3D and make the illustration world class. Empirical probe overturned the 'WebGL experimental' assumption: SwiftShader/ANGLE renders a full PBR frame at 2160x2700 in ~70ms in this container.
entries.change: Film-grade post pipeline: new assets/js/akpost.js implementing the researched minimal grading stack in the correct op order (bloom in linear, exposure, saturation, log-contrast pivot 0.18, ACES, gamma, lift/gain split-tone, luminance-masked soft-light grain, IGN dither as the banding killer, unsharp output sharpening) plus AKPOST.depthGrade (atmospheric-perspective numbers for layered 2D scenes).
entries.verification: Graded flagship proof slide (bloom catches the emissive beacon only, grain shaped into shadows, no banding on the night sky at 1:1); qa PASS; ~0.9s at 2160x2700 within the render budget.

entries.trigger: Maintainer directive (2026-07-11 dev session): take the studio from 2D to 3D and make the illustration world class. Empirical probe overturned the 'WebGL experimental' assumption: SwiftShader/ANGLE renders a full PBR frame at 2160x2700 in ~70ms in this container.
entries.change: OKLCH color engine: new assets/js/akcolor.js (sRGB<->OKLab/OKLCH, material ramps with the chroma bell + warm-light/cool-shadow hue drift, OKLab gradient mixing with no gray dead zone, gradient-map LUT underpainting, gamut mapping by chroma reduction at constant L/H).
entries.verification: Ramp/mix/LUT outputs spot-checked for gamut validity (all emitted hex parse and round-trip); used in the graded proof deck; loads clean (node --check + in-page).

entries.trigger: Maintainer directive (2026-07-11 dev session): take the studio from 2D to 3D and make the illustration world class. Empirical probe overturned the 'WebGL experimental' assumption: SwiftShader/ANGLE renders a full PBR frame at 2160x2700 in ~70ms in this container.
entries.change: Doctrine + library wired to the new bench: TECHNIQUE_LIBRARY entries 87-90 (GPU PBR Scene, SDF Raymarched Hero, Film Grade Pass, OKLCH Ramps + Gradient-Map Underpainting) with entry 49 superseded; DESIGN_DOCTRINE section 4 gains THE RENDERED LADDER (rendered hero + graded finish = the new craft floor for hero slides); SKILL.md documents the libraries, the pixelRatio rule, the sentinel, and the fallback mandate; examples/proof-3d committed as the 3-slide engine proof.
entries.verification: proof-3d renders+qa PASS from its committed location; knowledge files lint clean (no dashes).

entries.change: Enforcement wiring for the 3D bench: render.py extracts per-canvas telemetry (backing resolution + downsampled pixel variance); qa.py gains two hard gates: DEAD CANVAS (a visible canvas >= 25% of the slide with near-uniform pixels = failed GL frame or empty art layer -> FAIL) and LOW-RES BACKING (< 1.5x the 2x contract -> FAIL, 1.5-1.9x WARN); akthree sets preserveDrawingBuffer and webglOK now probes a throwaway canvas (probing the render target froze context attributes and blinded the sampler, found by the gate's own reconstruction run); scoring rubric's artwork-craft descriptors and the pixel-critic agent now name THE RENDERED LADDER so the taste gates enforce it; master prompt Phase 7 names the new gates.
entries.trigger: Maintainer question (2026-07-11): 'did you wire that all into the eval gates so that its all enforced?' The bench shipped opt-in; the failure modes it guards against (black GL frames, silent 1x renders) had no machine gate.
entries.verification: Reconstructions FAIL as required (dead-GL slide: 'dead canvas variance 0'; 1x slide: 'low-res canvas backing 1x', qa exit 1). Regression: examples/proof-3d PASS (after the probe-pollution fix the gate itself caught), examples/demo-deck baseline WARN unchanged, runs/2026-07-11 PASS. No gate weakened; two added.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-11) commit 4afc86c on PR #26 (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.change: New akthree helper AKT.objectHero(R, group, {toward, keyColor, intensity, height}) (plus standalone AKT.fitHeight): scales a foreground hero to a target world height (the framing bump) and adds a separation rim light on the FAR side of the subject from the camera, leaned toward the key direction, aimed at the subject center, so a dark object reads as a carved SILHOUETTE against a dark background instead of a flat blob. Documented in SKILL.md under the akthree bench. Opt-in; existing scenes unaffected.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-12 weakest scorer criterion (Artwork craft & genuine detail = 7): the S6 akthree object hero (backlit quadcopter) rendered as a blob under key+fill+ambient and only read after a warm rim from the light direction + a scale bump were added BY HAND (out/2026-07-12/slides/slide-06.html lines 111,125). A key-side rim alone leaves the profile flat; this commits the proven hand-fix as reusable machinery so every future object hero clears the RENDERED LADDER floor.
entries.verification: Clean A/B on a reconstructed backlit dark drone, identical base rig, the ONLY difference the objectHero rim: control has zero contour-separation (0 warm-edge px, peak region luminance = background 28.4) while the helper introduces a warm separation rim (37 warm-edge px, peak edge luminance 62.5, +120% over bg) confirmed visually and by a PIL luminance sampler using qa.py's method; AKT.fitHeight verified scaling the silhouette footprint 79k -> 381k px (~4.8x). Rim magnitude is intensity-tunable and framing-dependent by design (verified per slide by the existing RENDERED LADDER pixel gate). Regression: this run's 9 slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 (slide-06 imports the changed akthree.js and is unchanged) and examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (the known slide-03 busy-art warn), both identical to baseline; node --check clean. No gate weakened; no new runtime dependency (uses the committed three.module.min.js); shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-12) commit 01a43fb (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.change: Phase 3 and Phase 10 of the master routine now state explicitly that the fact-checker and scorer subagents have NO Write tool by design and return their JSON in the final message, which the showrunner persists to claims.json / score_report.json (replacing the '-> out/<date>/claims.json' and 'Save score_report.json' phrasing that implied the agent writes the file).
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-12 contract mismatch: the fact-checker (tools: WebFetch, Read) and scorer (tools: Read) cannot write files, so the showrunner had to persist their returned JSON by hand; the Phase 3/10 wording implied the agent writes it, a recurring low-risk surprise for any future showrunner. No defect shipped; documentation-correctness fix only.
entries.verification: Confirmed against .claude/agents/fact-checker.md (tools: WebFetch, Read) and scorer.md (tools: Read): neither has Write, and both agent prompts already end 'Your final message is this JSON', so the routine wording was the sole inaccuracy. Prose-only change to the spec doc; no engine/script/gate touched, nothing to re-render.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-12) commit 01a43fb (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.change: render.py's font-loaded QA probe now includes the element's computed font-style, so it tests the FACE ACTUALLY USED: it builds the document.fonts.check() spec as '<style> <weight> 32px "<family>"' (normalizing 'oblique 14deg' -> 'oblique') and keys its per-family dedup on style too, so an italic-only display face is checked against its italic @font-face instead of a hardcoded upright-400; the fonts_missing record and qa.py's FAIL message now carry the style ('font not loaded: <family> w<weight> italic'). Still FAILs a genuinely missing/unloaded declared face; no threshold changed.
entries.trigger: Recurring false-FAIL (flagged/parked 2026-07-10, touched 2026-07-11, recurred this run): Instrument Serif italic used as the SOFT/accent voice on S3/S6/S7/S9 tripped qa.py 'font not loaded: Instrument Serif w400' because the upright-400 probe tested the normal @font-face (unloaded when only italic text is present). The showrunner had to inject hidden offscreen upright-Instrument-Serif loader spans in 4 slides (slide-01.html:22 .isload; slide-06/07/09 left:-9999px loader spans) purely to satisfy the probe -- a hack this fix removes the need for. Frontier corroboration: MDN FontFaceSet.check treats font-style as a matching axis (a normal-style query matches only the normal @font-face). https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/FontFaceSet/check
entries.verification: Reconstruction slide using ONLY Instrument Serif italic (no offscreen upright loader): PRE-FIX qa FAILs 'font not loaded: Instrument Serif w400' (fonts_missing [{Instrument Serif,400}]); POST-FIX qa PASSes 0/0 (fonts_missing []). Non-weakening negative: a DECLARED italic @font-face pointing at a missing .ttf still FAILs 'font not loaded: Broken Face w400 italic'. Regression: this run's out/2026-07-13/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 (font verdict unchanged), examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (the known busy-art warn, font verdict unchanged). render.py in-page JS + qa.py both syntax-clean (node --check / ast.parse). No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency; shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: pending upgrade(2026-07-13) commit (showrunner stages this set)

entries.change: Added a FAILURE PROTOCOL clause codifying the account usage / weekly-limit fallback: on a 'You've hit your weekly limit'-class subagent error, treat the whole subagent layer as terminal for the run (every other subagent will fail identically), do NOT respawn or fan out further, switch immediately to solo showrunner execution (research, fact-check, treatment, copy, pixel review by reading each render PNG and the contact sheet, and scoring in the main loop), hold the score conservatively for the missing independent critic pass, disclose the degraded pass in the Gmail draft, and protect the Gmail deliverable by not spending budget on doomed fan-outs or discretionary scans.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-14: all six Phase-2 scout subagents died on 'Agent terminated early due to an API error: You've hit your weekly limit, resets 5pm (UTC)'; the six parallel spawns burned budget before the terminal signal was clear. The showrunner then shipped Carousel No. 7 solo. This makes the already-correct fallback explicit and prevents future budget waste on re-spawns.
entries.verification: Prose clause in the FAILURE PROTOCOL; no engine/script code changed, so the render.py + qa.py verification bar is satisfied vacuously (this run's slides and examples/demo-deck both rendered and QA-passed cleanly during the normal pipeline). Re-read the clause for correctness; weakens no gate, threshold, or hard-fail rule (it only removes wasted retries).
entries.rollback: Revert this upgrade commit; the FAILURE PROTOCOL returns to its prior three bullets. Behavior is unchanged in healthy runs.

entries.change: Two maintainer-directed fixes. (1) docket_alerts.py now reads the Buttondown API key from BUTTONDOWN_API_KEY, then Buttondown, then BUTTONDOWN (the deployed env var is named 'Buttondown'), so subscriber alerts stop SKIPping when the key is present. (2) Reversed the earlier 2026-07-14 FAILURE PROTOCOL clause (commit 524164d): account usage / weekly-limit subagent errors are now handled by WAITING for the reset and RESUMING the full multi-agent pipeline (run_state is resumable), never by degrading to a solo showrunner run or a reduced deck. The solo-degraded-run instinct was reframed to match.
entries.trigger: Maintainer instruction 2026-07-14: respawn agents if they die, no more degraded runs, wait for the usage limit to reset then continue; and the Buttondown API key IS in env (the 07-14 run SKIPped alerts only because the var is named Buttondown, not BUTTONDOWN_API_KEY).
entries.verification: docket_alerts.py parses (ast.parse OK); key detection verified in isolation against the deployed env (detected True, source 'Buttondown', 36 chars) with NO live send performed. Prompt and instinct edits reviewed for correctness; weakens no gate, threshold, or hard-fail rule.
entries.rollback: Revert this commit; docket_alerts returns to BUTTONDOWN_API_KEY-only and the FAILURE PROTOCOL returns to the solo-degraded fallback of 524164d.

entries.change: Refined the FAILURE PROTOCOL to be cause-based AND split out the fan-out guard as a standalone hard rule. (a) NON-NEGOTIABLE 7 now states subagent spawning is bounded and showrunner-only (fixed planned set per phase; subagents are leaf workers that never spawn subagents; never fan out beyond the plan on the showrunner's own initiative), independent of any failure. (b) Failure handling stays cause-based: wait for reset ONLY on a usage/rate/limit error then resume; for any other failure just respawn the SAME failed agent (about a 3-attempt cap), never adding new agents.
entries.trigger: Maintainer 2026-07-15: only wait if the failure was due to a limit, otherwise just respawn the failed agent; and the original ~20-agent runaway was an agent that CHOSE to spawn 20 more on its own (no failure involved), so the fan-out cap must be a standalone rule, not tied to failure handling.
entries.verification: Prompt and instinct prose reviewed for correctness and internal consistency; no code, gate, threshold, or hard-fail rule changed.
entries.rollback: Revert this commit; the FAILURE PROTOCOL returns to the 2026-07-14 wait-for-any-limit wording.
entries.commit: 8689cfc

entries.change: New scripts/copy_sync_check.py plus routine wiring (Phase 8 post-hand-edit pre-flight + Phase 11 completion-gate guard): for every slide string in copy.json['slides'] it verifies the string is present in that slide's RENDERED text (render_report.json text_nodes[].text). One-directional (authored copy must not go stale vs the render); alphanumeric-only, case/whitespace/punctuation-insensitive matching; long strings matched on their leading 40 alnum chars to tolerate render.py's .slice(0,80) node-text truncation, short display strings (the record-sync risk class) in full. Reads only; never edits copy.json or slides.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-17 record-sync gap: the showrunner hand-edited slide-05's kicker in the HTML ('HOW IT STARTED' -> 'BEFORE THE CLASS') after a flow-critic note, but copy.json went stale (still 'HOW IT STARTED') until the scorer's transcription caught it late. There was no machine check that copy.json matches the shipped render text.
entries.verification: Clean deck PASSes (exit 0): 52 authored slide strings all present in the render, 0 false positives. Defect reconstruction (S5 kicker reverted to the stale 'HOW IT STARTED') FAILs (exit 1) and reports exactly 'S5 kicker.text -> HOW IT STARTED'; an independent body-lead divergence reconstruction also FAILs correctly. Engine untouched and re-verified: qa.py on out/2026-07-17/render unchanged (WARN, 0 fails); examples/demo-deck render+qa unchanged (known slide-03 busy-art WARN, exit 0). No gate/threshold weakened, no new runtime dependency (stdlib only), shipped slides not modified; script parses (ast.parse OK).
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-17) commit (deletes scripts/copy_sync_check.py and the two Phase 8 / Phase 11 prompt lines; the pipeline reverts to no record-sync check)

entries.change: copy_sync_check.py now accepts copy.json['slides'] in EITHER shape: a dict keyed 'S1'.. (the record form) OR a list of per-slide objects (the copywriter / Phase 6 form). A new normalize_slides() keys a list into the 'S<n>' dict on each item's integer 'n' field (1-based position fallback) before any comparison, in both check() and main()'s orphan scan. Matching logic is unchanged; non-rendered bookkeeping fields (claim_ids etc.) were already in SKIP_KEYS and still cause no miss.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-18 contract mismatch: the copywriter agent + Phase 6 emit copy.json['slides'] as a LIST of {n, kicker, headline, body, labels, chips, claim_ids}, but copy_sync_check (authored 2026-07-17, only ever verified against that day's DICT-form copy.json) calls slides.items() and crashed with "AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'items'" on the real list-form artifact; the showrunner hand-restructured copy.json to a dict to run the Phase 8 / Phase 11 guard. Every list-form run defeats the record-sync net until manually converted. Weakens no gate: it only makes the existing guard runnable on the artifact it guards.
entries.verification: Pre-fix HEAD version on a faithful list-form reconstruction of this run's copy.json CRASHES (AttributeError at slides.items(), exit 1). Post-fix: (1) DICT form (shipped out/2026-07-18/copy.json) PASSes exit 0 -- 59 authored strings, backward compat intact; (2) LIST form PASSes exit 0 -- same 59 strings, so normalization is faithful; (3) defect reconstruction (list form with S3 kicker + S3 chip made stale) FAILs exit 1 and reports exactly the S3 kicker and 'chips[1]' misses on the correct slide -- the gate still catches drift. Engine untouched and re-verified: out/2026-07-18/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0; examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (known slide-03 busy-art warn, exit 0), both unchanged. No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency (stdlib only); ast.parse OK; shipped slides not modified.

entries.change: gmail_draft.py now falls back post_copy -> caption when 'post_copy' is absent, and synthesizes a default 5-item aftercare checklist (from CAROUSEL_CRAFT cadence/golden-hour rules: Tue-Thu 8-11am AKT, sources comment within 60s, golden-hour replies, saves>comments>shares>likes vs trailing median, evergreen daily-draft cadence) when 'aftercare' is absent, so the Gmail draft's paste-ready post block and aftercare section are never empty. Reads only; never mutates copy.json.
entries.trigger: Recurring gap logged 2026-07-17 and 2026-07-18 and again this run: the copywriter/Phase 6 emit 'caption' and no 'aftercare', but gmail_draft.py read copy.get('post_copy') and copy.get('aftercare'), so both draft blocks rendered EMPTY unless the showrunner hand-added post_copy and aftercare to copy.json (done by hand a 3rd time this run). This is the bounded permanent fix.
entries.verification: On a scratch copy of out/2026-07-19 with 'post_copy' and 'aftercare' REMOVED (caption kept), gmail_draft.py renders the caption text into the post-copy block and all 5 default aftercare items (aftercare <li> block non-empty). On the healthy shipped copy.json (post_copy + authored 4-item aftercare present) it uses the authored values and does NOT inject the default ('Drafts arrive daily' present, 'Treat the deck as evergreen' absent) -> fallback fires only on absence. Shipped copy.json not touched (tested on scratch copies). ast.parse OK. No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency.
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-19) commit (gmail_draft.py returns to copy.get('post_copy','') and copy.get('aftercare',[]) with no fallback)

entries.change: render.py adds a page.add_init_script hook (a self-invoking IIFE, runs before all slide scripts) that wraps CanvasRenderingContext2D fillText/strokeText and records every non-empty drawn string (deduped, into render_report.slides[].canvas_text); qa.py emits a WARN when a slide draws MEANINGFUL canvas text (>= 4 alphabetic chars, so axis ticks / short unit labels / bare numbers do not trip it), because canvas text is a raster bitmap invisible to render.py's DOM walk, to copy_sync_check, to the LinkedIn ranker, and to accessibility, and it pixelates in the vector PDF. WARN only; never a FAIL.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-19 gate blind spot: S7 loop labels and S8 annotations were drawn with canvas cx.fillText; they would have shipped as raster text in the vector PDF and copy_sync only caught the ones that were authored copy.json strings. Meaningful-but-unauthored canvas annotations were seen by NO machine gate. Fixed mid-run by converting to DOM. Frontier corroboration (headless-rendering scan): Playwright add_init_script is the sanctioned pre-page-script hook (LambdaTest/Apify docs) and MDN/accessibility sources confirm canvas fillText is raster, inaccessible, and non-scalable. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Canvas_API/Tutorial/Drawing_text
entries.verification: Reconstruction slide drawing cx.fillText('TRAINING LOOP')/strokeText('INFERENCE')/fillText('Model retrains nightly on new data') WARNs on all three (canvas raster text ...), while decorative fillText('2024'/'MW'/'42'/'GW') on the same and a control slide stay SILENT (the >=4-alpha filter). Regression (hook live): out/2026-07-19/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 with canvas_text empty on every slide (no false positives), and examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (only the known slide-03 busy-art warn, exit 0) with canvas_text empty -- both identical to baseline. Caught a real bug during verification: a bare arrow-expression add_init_script is injected but never called, so the IIFE form was required (empty capture on the first pass proved the hook was inert). ast.parse OK. No gate/threshold weakened (WARN only); no new runtime dependency (Playwright add_init_script already available); shipped slides/render not modified (verified in scratch dirs).
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-19) commit (removes CANVAS_TEXT_HOOK_JS + the add_init_script call + the canvas_text capture in render.py and the canvas-raster-text WARN loop in qa.py; the pipeline reverts to no canvas-text signal)
entries.commit: pending upgrade(2026-07-19) commit (showrunner stages this set)

entries.change: gmail_draft.py now resolves the scorer verdict fields through a local _alias() helper that accepts EITHER key spelling: ship via 'ship'||'ships', threshold via 'threshold'||'ship_threshold', and the weakest criterion via 'weakest_criterion'||'weakest_criteria'[0]. An agent-native score_report (which emits 'ships'/'ship_threshold'/'weakest_criteria') now renders the correct 'Quality gate passed' banner with the real threshold instead of the wrong 'Shipped below threshold ... vs ?. Weakest: ?' banner. Reads only; never mutates score_report.json.
entries.trigger: Reactive gap logged 2026-07-19 and hit again this run: the scorer agent's native JSON uses 'ships', 'ship_threshold', 'weakest_criteria' (a list), but gmail_draft.py read only 'ship', 'threshold', 'weakest_criterion' (singular), so score.get('ship',False) resolved False on an agent-native report and the draft rendered the orange below-threshold banner even though the deck shipped above threshold. The showrunner worked around it BOTH runs by hand-writing both key spellings into score_report.json (this run's out/2026-07-20/score_report.json carries both). This is the bounded permanent fix.
entries.verification: On a score_report with ONLY agent-native keys (ships:true, ship_threshold:8.3, weakest_criteria:[...]): gmail_draft.py renders 'Quality gate passed: 8.55 / 10 (threshold 8.3)' and NO 'Shipped below threshold' banner; the pre-fix logic (score.get('ship',False)) resolved False -> wrong below-threshold banner with threshold '?' and weakest '?' (defect reproduced). Below-threshold agent-native (ships:false) renders 'Shipped below threshold. 7.9 / 10 vs 8.3. Weakest: Artwork craft & genuine detail' -> weakest_criteria[0] and ship_threshold resolved correctly. Regression: this run's ACTUAL dual-spelling out/2026-07-20/score_report.json still renders 'Quality gate passed' with no wrong banner. Engine untouched (git diff empty on render.py/qa.py); examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (known slide-03 busy-art warn, exit 0) and out/2026-07-20/render qa WARN 0 fails, both baseline-unchanged. ast.parse OK. No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency; score_report.json not mutated (tested on scratch copies).
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-20) commit (gmail_draft.py returns to score.get('ship',False) / score.get('threshold','?') / score.get('weakest_criterion','?') with no aliasing)
entries.commit: pending upgrade(2026-07-20) commit (showrunner stages this set)

entries.change: AKT.snapshot (the akthree black-frame sentinel) gains a second, purely-additive COVERAGE accept path: after the historic 24-sample mean/variance read it does one full-buffer readPixels and counts strided lit pixels (luminance > litFloor=12), and returns ok=true if EITHER the mean/variance path passes OR the lit-pixel count clears LIT_MIN (max(48, 0.08% of samples)). It now returns {ok, variance, litCount} and accepts opts {litFloor, litMin, stride}. This fixes the silent false-fail where an object hero that fills only part of the frame over a transparent/dark empty background read mean/variance ~0 at the 24 sparse points and was wrongly judged a dead GL frame, forcing the flat Canvas fallback. The dead-canvas contract is preserved: a genuinely black/empty frame has litCount 0 AND fails mean/variance, so it still returns ok=false.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-21 primary reactive scar (chronic flat-hero / artwork-craft=7): S6's akthree GPU beluga composited over a transparent background as a subject filling under ~half the frame; the sentinel's 24 random samples mostly hit empty pixels so mean/variance read ~0 and .ok returned false, silently forcing the designed Canvas fallback. The Canvas beluga shipped and reads flat, capping artwork-craft at 7. The sentinel could not distinguish a real minority-coverage subject from a dead frame.
entries.verification: Defect reconstruction with two headless WebGL slides that surface shot.ok/litCount/variance into the DOM (read back from render_report.json text_nodes). (a) REAL SUBJECT: a lit hero filling only a compact center region that the 24 deterministic sample points all miss (nearest point ~182 buffer-px from center) -- PRE-FIX ok=0 var=0.00 (false-fail, indistinguishable from dead), POST-FIX ok=1 litCount=462 var=0.00 (LIT_MIN=73 at 91260 samples, 6.3x margin). (b) DEAD FRAME: empty scene, black clear, nothing drawn -- ok=0 litCount=0 both pre and post (contract preserved). Regression: examples/proof-3d (akthree slides 01/03) render OK + qa PASS 0/0 unchanged; examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (known slide-03 busy-art warn) unchanged; this run's out/2026-07-21/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 unchanged. node --check akthree.js OK. No gate/threshold weakened (accept path is purely additive; a dead frame still fails); no new runtime dependency (uses the committed three.module.min.js + WebGL readPixels already in use); shipped slides not modified (all tests in scratch dirs).
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-21) commit (AKT.snapshot returns to the 24-sample mean/variance-only sentinel returning {ok, variance})
entries.commit: 63fed63

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/akrelief.js: AK.reliefShade(cx, opts) turns a flat filled region into a shaded dimensional SOLID entirely in 2D ImageData, zero new deps. It builds a heightfield (noise.js fbm2/warp2 by default, or a height(u,v) callback for a dome/bevel/ridge), derives per-pixel surface normals from a 3x3 Sobel of the height, Lambert-shades against a single NW key (default) or the multidirectional oblique-weighted MDOW light set (azimuths 225/270/315/360 via {multidirectional:true}), maps a shadow->lit color ramp, and applies an optional silhouette mask (AK.reliefDome/reliefRect built in). This is the CPU/SVG-adjacent analogue of akthree.objectHero's rim-carve, so a LAND/object hero (the cadastral-plate / boreal-relief beat) stops reading flat. Documented in SKILL.md. Opt-in and unreferenced by any existing slide; text stays DOM/SVG and quantity is never encoded in the relief.
entries.trigger: Recurring artwork-craft=7 ceiling TWO runs running (2026-07-21 beluga fell back to a flat Canvas 2D; 2026-07-22 the raised cadastral plate was a flat SVG polygon that did not read as raised even with two stacked drop-shadows, and the low-poly AK3D heightfield read coarse under zoom). The scorer's one_sentence_fix both runs was 'build the hero as a real raised/relit solid.' Builds the helper PARKED at FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-21 (2.5D relit-heightfield Sobel form-shading). Frontier corroboration (this run's editorial-cartography scan): terrain/relief reads richer under multidirectional oblique-weighted hillshade (MDOW) than a single harsh light, so the helper ships an MDOW option. Sources: https://www.maptiler.com/news/2026/02/multidirectional-hillshades-and-terrain-color-ramps-for-web-maps/ , https://gist.github.com/maning/28ad9ebb1dcb1ea85440 , https://github.com/mattdesl/lwjgl-basics/wiki/ShaderLesson6
entries.verification: Two smoke slides through render.py + qa.py (scratch dirs). (1) NOISE relief field (built-in fbm/warp, strength 3, dome mask): renders OK 0 errors ~2.5s, qa PASS 0/0; the shaded region carries luminance std 53.2 (min 25 / mean 142 / max 215) vs ~0 for the flat fill it replaces, and reads visually as a textured relief boulder with real micro self-shadow. (2) SMOOTH DOME height (hemisphere, strength 90, NW key el 35): renders OK 0 errors ~0.7s, qa PASS 0/0; luminance std 58.4 and a clear directional terminator (NW rim 215 vs SE rim 82, NW/SE ratio 2.62), reads visually as a lit raised sphere. Regression (new file is unreferenced): examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (the known slide-03 busy-art warn, unchanged) and out/2026-07-22/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 (unchanged); git status shows only the new untracked asset, no engine/script/gate/slide modified. node --check akrelief.js OK. No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency (pure ImageData math over the committed noise.js).
entries.rollback: revert the upgrade(2026-07-22) commit (deletes assets/js/akrelief.js and the SKILL.md entry; the pipeline reverts to no 2D form-shading helper)

KEY: these primitives are ALREADY committed in noise.js (AK.fbm2/fbm3/warp2/simplex2), so a strata-texture/rim-light craft helper is a thin painterly layer over existing math, not new deps; relief needs only a lighter upper-left rim stroke on filled shapes plus low-alpha clipped fbm mottling.

scan_log.found: LinkedIn in 2026 has no native organic image carousel; swipeable posts are document uploads that LinkedIn RE-RENDERS into images, so embedded PDF/UA tags do NOT reach the LinkedIn viewer -- the reach/accessibility lever is the per-slide ALT TEXT typed into LinkedIn's upload UI, not the PDF. WCAG/PDF-UA tagged-PDF matters only for the public-site PDF (alaskaaihq.com), where assemble.py already sets Title/Author/Creator but no /Lang or Marked flag. Full PDF/UA tagging of vector-drawn pages is large and unbounded and not recommended.

scan_log.found: MDN FontFaceSet.check confirms font-style is a matching axis (a normal-style query matches only the normal @font-face) -- the mechanism behind this run's reactive fix. Playwright v1.49 REMOVED the old Chromium headless mode; screenshots render differently under the new headless mode, and Playwright ships chromium-headless-shell to preserve old-mode fidelity (install --only-shell). Determinism/regression risk for this engine's PNGs whenever Playwright is upgraded.
scan_log.outcome: applied (the font-style probe fix, corroborated by the MDN check() semantics) + parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-13: pin chromium-headless-shell / re-baseline screenshots on any Playwright upgrade, source URLs). scan_log.found: 2026 editorial maps converge on MINIMALIST: calm background (warm light grey / soft beige / very pale blue), one-to-two accent colors for the focal region, LIGHT relief (soft gradients / faint contour-inspired lines / simple water texture) over loud hillshade, label only narrative-referenced features, a small locator inset highlighting the focal region, short annotations. News-map content analysis (Designing Maps in News Stories) quantifies real-desk convention prevalence: scale bar 31.2%, inset 28.1%, legend 25%, north arrow 18.8%. Sources: https://www.onestopmap.com/blog/minimalist-editable-vector-maps/ , https://www.researchgate.net/publication/405348439_Designing_Maps_in_News_Stories_A_Longitudinal_Visual_Content_Analysis_of_Cartographic_Design_in_US_Data_Journalism

scan_log.found: The document-carousel strategy is REVALIDATED: PDF/document posts remain the highest-reach native format (~6.60% engagement; 3-6x text/image/video), and the March 2026 Authenticity Update + the 360Brew ranking model reward topical depth, real dwell time, and honest non-baity copy -- exactly this studio's output. Current specs already compliant: 9 slides sits in the 8-10 sweet spot (reach drops after slide 10), 2160x2700 is the recommended portrait 4:5, the 80px safe zone beats the >=50px minimum. Two levers surfaced but are strategy/craft, not bounded machine changes: SAVES are now the dominant ranking signal (~5x a like, ~2x a comment) with infographics the most common top-1% format (28.6%) -> save-worthiness as an explicit storyboard-gate/scorer objective; and the external-link penalty escalated (~60% less reach with a link, residual penalty even for link-in-first-comment) -> a maintainer strategy note, since sources-in-comments is integrity-required. Sources: https://www.oktopost.com/blog/linkedin-carousel-pdf-best-practices/ , https://www.dataslayer.ai/blog/linkedin-algorithm-february-2026-whats-working-now
scan_log.outcome: parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-18: save-worthiness scorer/gate candidate + escalated external-link-penalty maintainer note, with source URLs). scan_log.focus: headless-Chromium / Playwright rendering capabilities (rotated off the last 3 foci: procedural-relief 2026-07-15, editorial-dataviz 2026-07-17, LinkedIn-platform 2026-07-18); chosen to serve this run's rendering-robustness theme (8/9 slides failed the first render on a parse-time getElementById-null; S7/S8 shipped raster canvas text)
scan_log.found: Playwright add_init_script runs before ALL page scripts and is the sanctioned mechanism to wrap CanvasRenderingContext2D methods (LambdaTest/Apify/Playwright docs) -- the exact hook this run's canvas-text WARN uses (note: a bare arrow-expression is injected but never invoked; an IIFE is required). MDN + accessibility sources confirm canvas fillText renders a raster image of text, is not exposed to assistive tech, and pixelates when enlarged, with SVG/DOM the resolution-independent, selectable alternative -- the rationale for WARNing on meaningful canvas text. getElementById-null on a parse-time classic script is a settled class with fixes defer / module (deferred by default) / DOMContentLoaded / script-after-element (MDN, DOM-debugging guides). Playwright 1.60/headless-shell notes reconfirm the 07-13 parked screenshot-determinism risk on any Playwright upgrade. Sources: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Canvas_API/Tutorial/Drawing_text , https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Document/getElementById , https://www.lambdatest.com/automation-testing-advisor/python/playwright-python-add_init_script
scan_log.outcome: applied (canvas-raster-text WARN, render.py+qa.py, the 2026-07-19 engine fix above). Parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-19): a slide-authoring contract rule + optional pre-flight lint for the parse-time getElementById-null / element-referenced-before-defined class (D1: 8/9 slides failed the first render), and a body_overflow diagnostic hint for the inline-appended-SVG baseline line-box (D3) -- both already hard-fail at render, so the bounded win is authoring guidance not a fragile static analyzer this run.

scan_log.focus: typography / layout craft (last touched 2026-07-09; distinct from the last four foci: procedural-relief 2026-07-15, editorial-dataviz 2026-07-17, LinkedIn-platform 2026-07-18, headless-Chromium 2026-07-19); chosen to serve this run's recurring text-legibility and composition issues (S6 headline over the ISOTYPE grid; bodies wrapping to 4 lines vs the 3-line target; legibility criterion 7)
scan_log.found: The settled dataviz fix for text over marks is a TEXT HALO: a contrasting outline behind glyphs (SVG stroke + paint-order='stroke fill', stroke-width ~0.12-0.18em, halo color picked from text luminance) so contrast depends on (text, halo) not the art beneath -- the exact vector-text analogue of the studio's canvas-only aklabel.js, and the missing piece for S6's headline-over-ISOTYPE-grid blind spot that both existing gates (text-vs-text 07-08, PNG busy-art 07-10) passed. Tabular lining figures (font-feature-settings 'tnum') align numerals in stat/all-cap settings (Datawrapper/Type Network). CSS text-wrap: balance/pretty RECONFIRMED to not guarantee headline line count nor prevent overflow and pretty ignores widows (Chrome/MDN Sept 2025), so aktype.js AK.fitText stays the correct mechanism. Sources: https://data.europa.eu/apps/data-visualisation-guide/text-halos , https://www.datawrapper.de/blog/fonts-for-data-visualization , https://developer.chrome.com/blog/css-text-wrap-pretty
scan_log.outcome: parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-20: SVG/DOM text-halo helper + text-over-SVG-mark QA warn candidate needing a mark-intent attribute to bound false positives + tabular-figures craft note, all with source URLs). No frontier code applied: they are improvements not reactive fixes, the daily 0-1 budget was taken by the reactive gmail_draft.py score-key fix, and the halo helper edits shared rendering craft wanting a multi-deck A/B.

scan_log.focus: generative/procedural art portable to offline Canvas/SVG (last touched 2026-07-11; distinct from the last three foci LinkedIn-platform 07-18, headless-Chromium 07-19, typography 07-20); chosen to attack this run's chronic flat-hero / artwork-craft=7 weakness from the Canvas-fallback side (S6's GPU beluga fell back to a flat 2D beluga)
scan_log.found: The settled offline way to make a flat 2D canvas hero read as sculpted VOLUME without a GPU: build a procedural heightfield (noise.js AK.fbm2/warp2, already committed), derive a per-pixel normal from a 3x3 Sobel of the height (N=normalize(-dH/dx,-dH/dy,1/strength)), then Lambert-shade in 2D (ambient + max(dot(N,L),0)*diffuse with a light-Z for roundness + optional attenuation). ~100 lines vanilla JS, zero new deps -- the exact 2D analogue of akthree.objectHero's rim-carve. Hardware normal-mapped sprite pipelines (SpriteIlluminator/PixiJS) were ruled out as GPU-shader + baked-texture, off-policy. Sources: https://github.com/mattdesl/lwjgl-basics/wiki/ShaderLesson6 , https://arxiv.org/pdf/2212.09692 , https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.15354
scan_log.outcome: parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-21: 2.5D relit-heightfield form-shading helper with concrete Sobel+Lambert parameters + source URLs). No frontier code applied: it is an improvement not a reactive fix, the daily 0-1 budget was taken by the reactive AKT.snapshot coverage fix, and it edits shared rendering craft wanting a multi-deck A/B (it should be designed together with the parked relief-depth helper 2026-07-15 and strata-texture helper 2026-07-11 as one form/relief module over noise.js).

scan_log.focus: editorial dataviz / cartography technique (news-graphics relief + cadastral maps) -- last touched 2026-07-17; distinct from the last three foci headless-Chromium 2026-07-19, typography 2026-07-20, generative/procedural-art 2026-07-21; chosen to serve this run's cadastral/plat/boreal-relief theme and to inform the relief-shading helper being built
scan_log.found: Relief rendering converged on two live threads. (1) MULTIDIRECTIONAL OBLIQUE-WEIGHTED hillshade (MDOW) is the settled way to make relief read rich without a single harsh light: combine hillshades from azimuths ~225/270/315/360 aspect-weighted (ArcGIS Terrain Tools origin; MapTiler 2026-02 ships 4-direction hillshade + terrain color ramps; Eduard Cloud v1.0 Oct 2025 does ML Swiss-style locally-adjusted illumination). Directly applied: the new akrelief.js ships an MDOW light-set option. (2) Editorial cadastral maps stay minimalist (parcel boundaries + one accent for the focal parcel; no legend/border louder than the data), reconfirming the studio's chip-attribution practice. Sources: https://www.maptiler.com/news/2026/02/multidirectional-hillshades-and-terrain-color-ramps-for-web-maps/ , https://gist.github.com/maning/28ad9ebb1dcb1ea85440 , https://eduard.earth/ , https://github.com/mattdesl/lwjgl-basics/wiki/ShaderLesson6
scan_log.outcome: applied (the akrelief.js form-shading helper above adopts the MDOW multi-azimuth light set as an option; single NW key remains the default for object roundness). Cadastral-minimalism finding reconfirmed existing practice, no change.
